review_english: Minor spoilers. For players who like the Middle Ages, this game can bring you a unique journey of hunting monsters. This is a plot-driven game. The main story begins with finding your daughter. Find clues in various areas and finally help your daughter overcome difficulties. There are many branch lines and are not repetitive. Most of them are interesting and short and have many deep stories. The plot of this game is relatively open. There are many branch choices in both the main line and the branch lines. Your choices will rewrite the fate of many people and even the local current situation will be changed due to your intervention. The huge story network sets the stage for players who love role-playing. The two DLCs are highly recommended. The stories of the two DLCs are very good. They far exceed the quality of the main body and are huge. The two DLCs can double the length of the game and also add many interesting mechanisms. Next is a complaint about The Witcher 3. When I first played this game, I was put off by the boring and slow-paced beginning. The character dialogues were slow and the text was long. It made me sleepy as a new player. Later, after playing it again, I was put off by the boring and slow-paced beginning. I found that the horse in this game is very difficult to ride and the combat system is also very confusing. Although it is not to the level of garbage, it is not interesting at all. Then the main storyline is something I have always wanted to complain about. It is a very annoying loop-type plot, that is, to do event A, you have to do event B, and to do event B, you have to do event C. Some of them have to continue to be looped. And because I am a person who is not very interested in medieval themes, this game gives me too little driving force, and there is no feeling of addiction at all. Re-acceleration when riding a horse. The repeated dodges during the fight started to make me tired. However, I still finished the game for a week. Final summary: It is a good story-driven game and has a lot of content, but the feel is not good and the pace is too slow. Personal rating 7 10

review:
Ведьмак 3 со всеми DLC — это лучшая RPG в истории.

Сама игра легендарна, но дополнения — это вообще разрыв. Каменные сердца подарили Ольгерда и Гюнтера о'Дима — шедевральный, мрачный сюжет, от которого мурашки по коже. А Кровь и Вино? Туссент настолько красивый, что ощущается как отдельная игра, а финал дает Геральту тот самый идеальный заслуженный покой. Это шедевр на века, 11 из 10.

Hearts of Stone gave Olgerd and Gunther O'Dimm a masterpiece, dark plot that will give you goosebumps. What about Blood and Wine? Toussaint is so beautiful that it feels like a separate game, and the ending gives Geralt that perfect, well-deserved peace. This is a masterpiece for the ages, 11 out of 10.

review:
As a book fan I knew this was going to be great, but I was wrong. It was absolutely INCREDIBLE!!!
I can't explain how much this game affected me. The story, the characters, the sceneries.. Simply astonishing.
It took me 6 year to finish the whole story including DLCs, but it was just because I did not want it to end (now it did and i don't know how to process that)
Maybe the combat was a little hard for me, anyway that's my fault as I played most of the game on "Death March" difficulty. At the same time I was too lazy to learn all about every aspect of the game. I was always a simple sword kinda gal, but it's really useful to learn all about alchemy, potions, bombs, signs, mutagens and really get into what kind of build you want IF you want to play at high difficulty. There's really not much I can say without spoilers. Only thing - i'm a big fan of Regis from books and it was a big pleasure to play Blood and Wine DLC with him in it.

review: Короче, Ведьмак 3 - это когда весь мир на грани уничтожения, твою приемную дочь преследуют призрачные всадники, а ты стоишь посреди грязной деревни и орешь на местного барона, потому что он не хочет играть с тобой в карты. Гвинт здесь - это вообще смысл жизни, а спасение человечества - так, приятный бонус. Отдельного уважения заслуживает Плотва: эта кобыла явно познала дзен и научилась телепортироваться на крыши домов, застревать головой в заборах и полностью игнорировать законы физики. Еще Геральт работает профессиональным синоптиком и постоянно бубнит под нос про дождь и ветер. При этом экономика в игре потрясающая: ты можешь легально обнести хату любого нищего крестьянина, забрав у него последний тухлый сыр и сломанную кочергу, и тебе ничего не будет. Но стоит случайно нажать кнопку атаки рядом со стражником в городе - и тебя сотрут в порошок за долю секунды. Ну и главный мем игры - это хрустальные колени великого ведьмака. Геральт прошел через жуткие мутации, побеждает огромных монстров и мастерски крутит пируэты с мечом, но гарантированно умирает, если упадет с высоты обычного забора. В общем, 10 шевелящихся Плотв из 10, шедевр на сотни часов, пойду соберу еще пару карт для колоды Нильфгаарда, пока дочка там как-нибудь сама справляется.

review_english: In short, The Witcher 3 is when the whole world is on the verge of destruction, your adopted daughter is being chased by ghostly horsemen, and you are standing in the middle of a dirty village yelling at the local baron because he doesn’t want to play cards with you. Gwent here is generally the meaning of life, and saving humanity is a nice bonus. Roach deserves special respect; this mare clearly knew Zen and learned to teleport to the roofs of houses, get her head stuck in fences and completely ignore the laws of physics. Geralt also works as a professional weather forecaster and constantly mutters under his breath about rain and wind. At the same time, the economy in the game is amazing; you can legally surround the hut of any poor peasant, taking away his last rotten cheese and broken poker, and you will have nothing. But if you accidentally press the attack button next to a guard in the city, you will be pulverized in a split second. Well, the main meme of the game is the crystal knees of the great witcher. Geralt went through terrible mutations, defeats huge monsters and masterfully pirouettes with a sword, but is guaranteed to die if he falls from the height of an ordinary fence. In general, 10 moving Roaches out of 10, a masterpiece for hundreds of hours, I’ll go and collect a couple more cards for the Nilfgaard deck, while my daughter somehow manages on her own.

review:
최근 게임 기준으로 해도 수작이라고 봄. 조작감 구리고 낡은 ui인 건 맞는데, 이게 약간 옛날 메이플 같은 맛이 있음. 불편해서 오히려 모험하는 느낌이 많이 든다 해야하나. 슥슥 넘어가면 할 땐 편하겠지만 1달만 지나가도 다 잊힐건데. 다만 동굴 탐험은 좀 빡침 맵이 어둡고 길이 잘 안 보임. 

서브퀘스트랑 탐험요소가 많기는 한데 메인퀘스트만 즐겨도 충분한 볼륨이 나옴. 그리고 게임 스토리 좋다는 경우 대부분이, 게임 치고 겸사겸사 좋은 거지 순수 문학 작품한테는 밀리는 경우가 많은데. 위쳐3는 원작 소설 뛰어넘었다고 봐야함. 다양한 선택지가 있고 캐릭터랑 세계가 살아숨쉬는 게 활자로 전달할 수 없는 몰입감이 있음.

review:
위쳐 시리즈를 먼저 소설로 접하고, 호수의 여인까지 다 본 독자로써 게임에 대한 기대감이 컸는데 그 기대감을 정말 잘 충족시켜준 게임, 후속작으로 나올 위쳐4가 정말 걱정이 될 정도로 잘 나온 작품. 
잦은 선택을 요하나 피곤하진 않은 수준이라 오히려 매력으로 다가왔고, 캐릭터 하나하나가 입체적이고 매력적이다.
아직도 해보지 않은 게이머가 있다면, 할인할 때 사면 그 몇 배 이상의 재미를 줄 수 있을 거라고 확신하는 작품.

Although it requires frequent choices, it is not tiring, so it is rather attractive, and each character is three-dimensional and attractive. review:
A phenomenal game with a grpping yet immersive story. The Witcher 3 will always remain one of my favourite and most memorable games I will ever play. Aside from the 10/10 gameplay, TW3 helped me navigate and persevere through a really difficult point in my life. 

What I appreciate the most about the game is how it, akin to the books, explores the complexities of the human psyche that is capable of great compassion and evil - both sides of the extreme and still everything in between. Yet the most poignant part is that such diversity of emotions and traits are not limited to certain steroetypes but are evidently hidden amongst all characters - from Geralt, the Bloody Baron, Olgierd, Detlaff/Syanna to emeperors, nobles down to peasants. The use of a world mirroring the political context of medieval Europe emphasises the toil everyone experiences, regardless of their socioeconomic background. 

Albiet being games from the same developer, I can't help but compare and contrast the Witcher 3 to Cyberpunk 2077 - both being my favourite games. Both of which serve as a pertinent commentary of humanity both in the past and what seems to be the looming future. 

Nonetheless, playing the Witcher 3 - from the combat to making morally-grey decisions on the spur of the moment - made me reflect a lot on the choices I've made in my life. In retrospect, we can never be too sure of the choices we make nor of the consequences they made have - on a single person or something much bigger. There is no right choice. 

“Mistakes,’ he said with effort, ‘are also important to me. I don’t cross them out of my life, or memory. And I never blame others for them.”

I understand not every game or genre is for everyone, but I do recommened giving TW3 and its DLCs a try. Corny I know, but it was life-changing for me.And hopefully it may make you feel something too :-)

review:
«Ведьмак 3» — это та самая игра, в которую стоит зайти хотя бы ради атмосферы. Что сразу цепляет, так это то, как прописан мир: он кажется живым, немного мрачным, но очень притягательным. Здесь даже самый мелкий побочный квест может превратиться в целую детективную историю с неожиданным финалом, где нет однозначного добра или зла.
Главный герой, Геральт, — фактурный персонаж, за судьбой которого реально интересно следить. Визуально игра до сих пор выглядит отлично: закаты в Велене или горы Скеллиге завораживают. Музыкальное сопровождение с фольклорными мотивами идеально дополняет картинку. Если вы любите долгие приключения, где ваши решения действительно влияют на мир и окружающих людей, то это определенно маст-хэв. Ну так что ? Партейку в гвинт ?)

review_english: The Witcher 3 is the game that is worth playing if only for the atmosphere. What immediately catches you is the way the world is written; it seems alive, a little gloomy, but very attractive. Here, even the smallest side quest can turn into a whole detective story with an unexpected ending, where there is no clear good or evil. The main character, Geralt, is a rich character whose fate is really interesting to follow. Visually the game still looks great; the sunsets in Velen or the Skellige Mountains are mesmerizing. Musical accompaniment with folklore motifs perfectly complements the picture. If you love long adventures where your decisions really impact the world and the people around you, then this is definitely a must-have. So what? Party for gwent?)

review:
One of the greatest games ever made! I have to say for a game that is over 10 years old the graphics still look absolutely stunning. The visuals, music, sound design and overall atmosphere are incredible and still hold up today. The story is amazing, the gameplay is fun and the characters are some of the best written characters I’ve ever seen in a game. The world feels alive and is full of interesting places, quests and secrets to discover. You will never run out of things to do and instead of getting bored you’ll probably end up spending dozens or even hundreds of hours exploring every part of the game and enjoying every minute of it. The DLCs are also fantastic and add dozens of hours of great content.. definitely worth playing! I honestly think this game is a true gem. I love pretty much every aspect of it, from the world and story to the gameplay and soundtrack. It’s one of those rare games that I can always come back to and enjoy just as much as the first time.

9.8/10

review:
Truly I cannot explain the masterpiece that is "Witcher 3: the Wild Hunt"... very near perfect game with very few flaws. This game is a excellent standalone game, but if you play the whole trilogy in order ("Witcher 1" and "Witcher 2: Assassins of Kings") this game becomes an extraordinary epic conclusion to the Witcher trilogy.

After two playthroughs, both combined taking over three hundred hours... I can confidently say Witcher 3 is one of my favorite games and will remain one of my favorite games, along with the other two prequels to this game.

This game has many good characteristics I could describe, but something that always stands out to me is the graphics/environments. This game was released in 2015 and the graphics are better than a lot of present day games. Not to mention the environments and scenery, absolutely stunning... From the beautiful landscapes of Toussaint to the snow capped peaks of the Skellige Isles... this game is visually stunning.

Lastly, while the base "Witcher 3: Wild Hunt" is spectacular, when mentioning this game you have to talk about the amazing DLCs. "Blood and Wine" & "Hearts of Stone" are two paid DLCs that are one hundred percent worth purchasing and fantastic additions to Witcher 3. Even if that doesn't suit your fancy, there are a ton of free DLCs that you can add or remove to your game as you please.

All in all, easily a 10/10.

review:
Наконец, спустя 11 лет после релиза, я добрался до этой игры, которую многие так любят и даже восхваляют. Много распинаться не буду, игра мне понравилась, но конечно прежде всего за мир и сюжет. Как бы то ни было, игровой процесс я считаю весьма средним с кривоватой реализацией. Это была хорошая история, которая в моих руках растянулась на полгода прохождения. Конечно, тут я ещё не закончил, ибо меня ждут ещё два дополнения, которые я решил пройти после окончания основного сюжета.

Если вы ещё не играли, то рекомендую вам это сделать, и вас наверняка затянет это приключение с головой.

review:
Прошёл на платину и вам советую — только так игра раскрывается на полную.

Сюжетные дополнения («Каменные сердца» и «Кровь и вино») здесь в разы круче и интереснее основного сюжета. А уж побегать и позачищать знаки вопросов на Скеллиге — ух, это именно то, что нужно для истинного кайфа под пивас. В игре есть квесты, где твой выбор реально имеет долгоиграющие последствия, и это очень гуд. Гвинт внутри игры — просто супер, но вот в отдельную игру «Гвинт» я вам соваться крайне не советую... В неё лучше вообще не играть, это совсем другая история.

Ведьмак выглядит потрясающе красиво даже 11 лет спустя. И оптимизация тут то что надо — не то что у Тодда Говарда с его загрузками по 5 минут между комнатами!

Если вы до сих пор не играли в этот шедевр — вы что, только что из пещеры вылезли и решили первым делом почитать отзывы? Шевелись, Плотва! Бегом играть!

review:
👍 Recommended
🕒 22.9 hours on record  

The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt is a true masterpiece and easily one of the best RPGs ever made. With 22.9 hours on record, I’ve only begun to scratch the surface of its massive world, but it’s already clear why this game is so highly regarded.

The story and characters are phenomenal. Geralt’s journey, the morally gray choices, and the deep, emotional side quests make the world feel alive and meaningful. Every decision actually matters, and the writing is top-tier across the board. The base game alone is incredible, but the two expansions (Hearts of Stone and Blood and Wine) are some of the best DLC ever released — especially Blood and Wine, which feels like its own full game.

Gameplay is engaging and rewarding. Combat feels satisfying once you get used to it, exploration is a joy thanks to the beautiful and varied world, and the side content is so good that you’ll often get more immersed in it than the main story. The atmosphere, music, and voice acting are all outstanding.

Even years later, The Witcher 3 holds up as a benchmark for open-world RPGs. It’s a long, rich adventure that respects your time and rewards curiosity.

If you haven’t played it yet, this is a must-play. One of the greatest games of its generation.  

**10/10** — A true masterpiece. 🗡️🐺❤️

review: Я возвращаюсь в эту игру уже который год, и она до сих пор умудряется пробирать до мурашек. Здесь потрясающий, живой мир, в котором хочется исследовать каждый куст, а не просто бегать по маркерам на карте. Но главное — это истории: даже второстепенные квесты написаны так круто и душевно, что заставляют искренне сопереживать персонажам. Музыка, атмосфера славянского фэнтези, харизматичный Геральт — всё это создаёт шедевр, который затягивает на сотни часов. Если вы каким-то чудом всё ещё прошли мимо этой легенды, то я вам искренне завидую. Рекомендую от всего сердца, 10 утопцев из 10.

review_english: I've been coming back to this game for years now, and it still manages to give me goosebumps. There is a stunning, living world here, in which you want to explore every bush, and not just run along the markers on the map. But the main thing is the stories, even the secondary quests are written so cool and sincerely that they make you sincerely empathize with the characters. The music, the atmosphere of Slavic fantasy, the charismatic Geralt all create a masterpiece that will captivate you for hundreds of hours. If by some miracle you still passed by this legend, then I sincerely envy you. I recommend it with all my heart, 10 drowners out of 10.

review:
慕名而来，但是玩了近50小时实在玩不下去了先说优点:美术非常漂亮，每个角色的建模都很精致好看，难以想象是近10年前的游戏然后开始说缺点。首先是游戏性，不好玩真的不好玩。作为主要玩法的战斗系统完全是依托构式，砍到怪完全没有什么反馈，那个法术放了也和没放没什么感受。怪只有被砍到没有反应和夸张的往回弹两种。感觉这游戏最好玩的是昆特牌然后是剧情，我没玩过巫师12(买了根本打不开游戏)，只在网上看了剧情介绍，但依旧打开一脸懵，好多人都不认识，也没有旁白介绍/背景介绍，直接默认我们很熟悉，极大削弱了代入感。然后剧情上也一直没有什么很吸引我的地方(听说dic剧情很神但是没能玩到了)。npc互动也很少，而且这游戏没有那种一直在一起的同伴，很多时候都是跑来跑去好不容易和一个人熟悉了又换地图见一堆不认识的人。跑图:为什么要和育碧学习地图上一堆问号，而且很多还是通马桶任务，但是一堆通马桶任务中又有一些有剧情的支线，根本无从分辨。地图上一堆问号看着让人很不爽。
总而言之实在没有办法为了“听说后期很神”的剧情坚持下去了。

review_english: I came here because of the reputation, but after playing for nearly 50 hours, I really can’t stop playing. Let’s talk about the advantages first. The art is very beautiful. The modeling of each character is exquisite and beautiful. It’s hard to imagine that this is a game nearly 10 years ago. Then let’s start talking about the shortcomings. The first is the gameplay. It’s not fun. It’s really not fun. I have never played The Witcher 12 (I couldn’t open the game after buying it). I only read the plot introduction online, but I was still confused when I opened it. I didn’t recognize many people, and there was no narration or background introduction. It just assumed that we were familiar with each other, which greatly weakened the sense of immersion. Then there was nothing very attractive about the plot (I heard that the plot of DIC was amazing, but I couldn’t play it). There was also very little NPC interaction, and this game didn’t have the kind of companions who are always together. Most of the time, I have to run around and finally get to know someone, and then I change the map to meet a bunch of people I don’t know. Why do I need to learn from Ubisoft? There are a lot of question marks on the map, and many of them are toilet clearing tasks. But among the bunch of toilet clearing missions, there are some sub-plots with plots that are impossible to tell. A bunch of question marks on the map look very uncomfortable. review:
好玩，非常的好玩，作为一款开放世界大地图游戏，它有着极其丰富的支线剧情任务以及的小事件剧情任务，大量的剧情内容让人可以更加沉浸地体会这个游戏世界，剧情中所触发出来的不同对话选项，每一个都代表着故事之后的不同走向，你的每一个选择都可能改变一个人或者更多人的命运，连带着影响后续故事的走向，你在任务中所做出的每一个决定可能会拯救或者害死更多或更少的人，要为了拯救一个人还是说大部分人从而牺牲一群或者一个人，选择杀掉还是拯救落单的逃兵，选择与人谈判或者使用迷惑还是说直接了当干掉对方，你的每一个举动都可能改变故事中那些人的命运，无论是利益主义之上选择金钱或者更多事不关己的选项也好，还是出于善心与正义选择多管闲事也罢，你想在这个故事中扮演一个怎样的主角由你自己决定，冷漠还是善良，多情还是专一，谈判还是消灭，就是因为这个游戏的剧情选项有着这样的诸多可能，所以才让人着迷，参与政治斗争，你能够改变一个甚至是好几个国家的命运，你一念之差所救或是放过的人，可能会为害一方，可能会对之后的你有所帮助，你无意间所做的一个决定可能会间接导致一群或者一个人的死亡，或者可能会拯救一个或是更多的人，所以每当我要做出选择的时候常常会陷入一种两难的境地，我应该相信吗，我应该放过吗，我应该拯救吗，他人所说的究竟是谎言还是真相，我究竟怎么做才是正确的，这样的纠结也是这个游戏的魅力，意味着你真的愿意把这个游戏的故事人物认真对待，决定这一切的选择就在你的手中，不然怎么会让人沉迷呢。
    战斗系统这方面也是简单好上手，炼金魔药增益帮助探索战斗，法印帮助战斗辅助或是是作为主要输出手段，看你的偏好选择技能点的加成，虽然说战斗方面没什么深度，前期凑合有点牢，但是发展到后期真的随便打，一剑一个怪，但是对我这种萌新玩家来说刚刚好，而且巫师三作为一款开放世界游戏，丰富的剧情才是这个游戏最大的卖点，战斗方面没什么深度也无可厚非，况且这对没怎么接触过单价游戏的玩家来说非常的友好，我认为这反而不是缺点而是优点，这能够吸引更多想体验剧情但是是手残的游戏玩家，没错，我就是其中之一，而且优化方面也做得不错，我的垃圾轻薄本竟然也能带得动，虽然画质基本算最低，但是玩起来还算流畅。
     这个游戏唯一的缺点就是在任务指引和一些地点标注方面做的不太行，除了一些固定的商人或是铸剑师和护甲匠有地图明显标注，其他很多商人和铸剑护甲地点是没有在地图上标出来的这你敢相信？因为他们有一些出现的时间是不固定的，你在他们原本的位置上出现有时候他们是不在的，你需要通过冥想调整时间才能够把他们刷新出来，还有一些商人地点是要你自己到附近了才会在地图上显示，真的特别操蛋，这里建议在游玩的过程中自己在地图上另外标注那些不显示的商人或者工匠的地点，不然到你想用的时候就找不到了。任务指引方面你如果没有在探寻范围找到足够有用的线索就不会指引出你接下来该走的路线，这其实还不算什么，不过只是提醒你要注意细节认真游戏，操蛋的是有时候目标物品或者是线索常常出现在任务指引探索范围边缘甚至是之外一段距离，疯狂在任务标注的探索范围内打转却毫无收获的绝望你们懂吗？所以说在探索任务线索或者任务道具奖励时也得多在标注范围外多走动才会有意外发现，不然可能会卡壳，如果范围外也找不到，那可能真的是你在范围内漏看了，实在不行就去找攻略。
    总而言之巫师三是一款非常优秀的，值得萌新小白或者是剧情党玩家游玩的游戏，丰富优秀的多结局剧情分支，简单好上手的战斗模式，战斗，打牌，逛窑子一应俱全，建议没体验过的人都可以上手玩玩，我当时买的时候还不是目前的最低价，但已经是当时的最低价了，而且只有本体不包含DLC，我向来没有本体加DLC一起买的习惯，但是真正体验完游戏之后就开始让我感到可惜了，之后有机会可能会补，但是就算没有DLC，本体就是相对一款完整的游戏了，所以也不亏，更何况是打折购入的，如果你没怎么玩过游戏，巫师三真的是一款非常好的入坑选择，无论打没打折都值得一玩，希望没有尝试过的人都能来玩一玩，你绝对不会失望的。

review_english: Fun, very fun. As an open world large map game, it has an extremely rich side plot mission and small event plot missions. The large amount of plot content allows people to experience the game world more immersed. Different dialogue options triggered in the plot each represent a different direction after the story. Each of your choices may change the fate of one or more people, and will also affect the direction of the subsequent story. Every decision you make in the mission may save or kill more or fewer people. To save one person or most people, sacrifice a group or one person. Choose to kill or save lone deserters. Choose to negotiate with others or use confusion or kill them directly. Every move you make may change the fate of those in the story. Whether you choose money or more irrelevant options out of selfishness, or choose to be nosy out of kindness and justice. It’s up to you what kind of protagonist you want to play in this story. Indifferent or kind, passionate or single-minded, negotiate or eliminate. It is because of the many possibilities in the plot options of this game that it makes people fascinated. Participate in political struggles and you can change the fate of one or even several countries. The people you save or spare in a single thought may harm one party and may help you in the future. Is what others say a lie or the truth? What is the right thing for me to do? This kind of entanglement is also the charm of this game. It means that you are really willing to take the story characters of this game seriously. The choice to decide all this is in your hands. Otherwise, how can you become addicted? The combat system is also simple and easy to use. The alchemy potion buff helps to explore the battle. The sigil can help combat support or be used as the main output method. Depending on your preference, choose the bonus of skill points. Although there is no depth in combat, it is a bit solid in the early stage. But in the later stages of development, you can really fight casually, one sword and one monster, but it is just right for a new player like me. Moreover, The Witcher 3 is an open world game, and the rich plot is the biggest selling point of this game. There is nothing wrong with the lack of depth in combat. Moreover, this is very friendly to players who have not had much exposure to single-price games. I think this is not a disadvantage but an advantage. This can attract more gamers who want to experience the plot but are handicapped. Yes, I am one of them, and I have also done a good job in optimization. My junk thin laptop can actually carry it. Although the picture quality is basically the lowest, it plays smoothly. The only shortcoming of this game is that the task guidance and some location markings are not very good. Except for some fixed merchants or swordsmiths and armorers, which are clearly marked on the map, many other merchants and sword and armor making locations are not marked on the map. Can you believe it? Because some of their appearance times are not fixed. Sometimes they are not there when you appear at their original location. You need to adjust the time through meditation to refresh them. There are also some merchant locations that will only be displayed on the map when you are nearby. It’s really fucking annoying. It is recommended that you mark the locations of those merchants or craftsmen that are not displayed on the map during the game. Otherwise, you won’t be able to find them when you want to use them. In terms of mission guidance, if you don’t find enough useful clues in the exploration range, it will not guide you on the route you should take next. This is actually nothing, but it just reminds you to pay attention to the details and play the game seriously. The damn thing is that sometimes the target items or clues often appear at the edge of the exploration range of the mission guide or even a distance away. Do you understand the despair of wandering around the exploration range marked by the mission but getting nothing? So when exploring mission clues or mission prop rewards, you have to move more outside the marked range to make unexpected discoveries. Otherwise, you may get stuck. If you can't find it outside the range, you may really have missed it within the range. If it doesn't work, go find the guide. All in all, The Witcher 3 is a very good game. A game worthy of being played by newbies or story-loving players. Rich and excellent plot branches with multiple endings. A simple and easy-to-learn combat mode. It has everything you need to fight, play cards, and visit brothels. It is recommended that anyone who has never experienced it can start playing it. When I bought it, it was not the lowest price at the time, but it was already the lowest price at that time. And only the main body did not include DLC. I have never been in the habit of buying the main body and DLC together, but after I really experienced the game, I started to feel regretful. I may buy it later if I have the opportunity. But even without the DLC, the main body is a relatively complete game, so it’s not a loss, not to mention buying it at a discount. If you haven’t played the game much, The Witcher 3 is really a very good choice to get into, whether it’s discounted or not, it’s worth playing. I hope people who haven’t tried it can give it a try. You won’t be disappointed.

review:
I genuinely don’t understand how this game is worshipped like some untouchable masterpiece. After dozens of hours, all I’m left with is frustration, boredom, and the feeling that I’ve been tricked by hype.

First of all, the combat is painfully clunky. It looks flashy at first, but after a few fights, you realize it’s the same repetitive dodge–hit–dodge loop with zero depth. Geralt moves like he’s constantly stuck in mud, and the hitboxes feel inconsistent. For a game centered around hunting monsters, the fighting is shockingly unsatisfying.

Then there’s the overhyped open world. Sure, it’s big. But big doesn’t mean good. Most of it is empty fields, copy-paste villages, and question marks that lead to the same bandit camp or loot chest over and over again. It quickly turns into a checklist simulator rather than an actual adventure.

The story pacing is a mess. People praise the narrative, but it’s bloated with filler quests that completely kill any sense of urgency. You’re supposedly chasing someone important, yet the game constantly distracts you with errands that feel meaningless. It’s like the main plot is fighting for attention against its own side content—and losing.

Also, the controls are awkward and unresponsive, especially on keyboard. Riding the horse is a chore, not a feature. Roach gets stuck on everything, refuses to follow paths properly, and turns simple travel into a headache.

And let’s not ignore the overly long dialogues. Yes, choices exist, but most conversations drag on forever and often don’t feel as impactful as they pretend to be. It’s quantity over quality.

Technically, the game still shows its age. Despite updates, there are bugs, weird physics, and animation issues that break immersion. For a title released back in 2015, you’d expect some polish after all these years, but it still feels rough in many areas.

In the end, this game feels like a perfect example of style over substance wrapped in nostalgia and hype. It’s not terrible—but it’s nowhere near the “best game ever made” status people keep throwing around.

Verdict: Overrated, bloated, and ultimately exhausting. Not recommended.

After dozens of hours, all I m left with is frustration, boredom, and the feeling that I ve been tricked by hype. First of all, the combat is painfully clunky. It looks flashy at first, but after a few fights, you realize it s the same repetitive dodge hit dodge loop with zero depth. Geralt moves like he s constantly stuck in mud, and the hitboxes feel inconsistent. For a game centered around hunting monsters, the fighting is shockingly unsatisfying. Then there s the overhyped open world. Sure, it s big. But big doesn t mean good. Most of it is empty fields, copy-paste villages, and question marks that lead to the same bandit camp or loot chest over and over again. It quickly turns into a checklist simulator rather than an actual adventure. The story pacing is a mess. People praise the narrative, but it s bloated with filler quests that completely kill any sense of urgency. You re supposedly chasing someone important, yet the game constantly distracts you with errands that feel meaningless. It s like the main plot is fighting for attention against its own side content and losing. Also, the controls are awkward and unresponsive, especially on keyboard. Riding the horse is a chore, not a feature. Roach gets stuck on everything, refuses to follow paths properly, and turns simple travel into a headache. And let s not ignore the overly long dialogues. Yes, choices exist, but most conversations drag on forever and often don t feel as impactful as they pretend to be. It s quantity over quality. Technically, the game still shows its age. Despite updates, there are bugs, weird physics, and animation issues that break immersion. For a title released back in 2015, you d expect some polish after all these years, but it still feels rough in many areas. In the end, this game feels like a perfect example of style over substance wrapped in nostalgia and hype. It s not terrible but it s nowhere near the best game ever made status people keep throwing around. Verdict Overrated, bloated, and ultimately exhausting. Not recommended.

review:
The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt is one of the best open-world RPG games ever made. The story follows Geralt of Rivia, a monster hunter searching for his adopted daughter in a dark and dangerous world.

The game has an incredible story, deep characters, and meaningful choices that really affect the outcome. The world feels alive, with beautiful landscapes, cities, forests, and monsters everywhere.

Combat is fun and strategic, mixing sword fighting with magic signs and potions. Side quests are not boring at all — many of them feel like full stories on their own.

The graphics and music are amazing, creating a strong atmosphere. Even years after release, the game still feels modern and high quality.

Overall, The Witcher 3 is a masterpiece and a must-play game for anyone who enjoys RPGs and story-driven games.

review:
Review – The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt Complete Edition (Steam)

After many years, The Witcher 3 still feels like one of the best RPGs ever made. Few games manage to combine storytelling, world-building, memorable characters, and meaningful side quests at this level.

👍 What I Loved

Outstanding story and characters. Geralt, Ciri, Yennefer, Triss, and many others are written incredibly well.
Side quests often feel better than main stories in other RPGs.
Choices have real consequences, sometimes hours later.
The world is beautiful and full of interesting stories to discover.
Hearts of Stone and Blood and Wine are among the best expansions ever released.

👎 What Could Be Better

Combat is serviceable but not the game's strongest feature.
Movement can feel a bit awkward at times.
Some points of interest, especially in Skellige, can become repetitive.
The first few hours may feel overwhelming due to the number of systems and quests.

⚖️ Final Verdict
The Witcher 3 is the complete package: an incredible story, memorable characters, meaningful choices, and hundreds of hours of content. Even years after release, it remains the benchmark for story-driven open-world RPGs.

Recommended 👍

10/10 – A masterpiece that every RPG fan should experience at least once. 🐺⚔️

"Evil is evil. Lesser, greater, middling... makes little difference."

review:
10/10

Deserving of all the hype and acclaim. The best Western RPG ever made. Just get it now before the DLC and play on Death March difficulty for a extremely immersive Witcher-sim, were every bomb, potion, and oil can be the difference between life or death. The star of the show is the world and atmosphere. It is so relaxing and immersive to just ride around, listening to all the dialogue and admiring the still stunning graphics and god-tier soundtrack. The game really makes you feel like a badass monster hunter, drifting though towns and cities looking for work.

The story is great, with the voice acting and dialogue in particularly being industry best. Everyone, even random NPC villagers in ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ nowhere, give great performances. The world is brutal and unforgiving, with many plots and quests having no real "everybody wins" option. People act logically and in their own interests and Geralt can choose how involved he becomes in the dilemmas of others. The side quests and contracts are also fantastic, with many RPG's know mimicking some of the elements from this game. The DLC's will be their own reviews, but both are great. With Blood and Wine being even better than the main story and featuring my personal favorite area/setting in any game.

The combat is more fun on the harder difficulties, but it does feel a little dated and can get repetitive. The abilities and loadout mechanics are also a little restrictive once you get to higher levels. But this is remedied by the DLCs, which introduce new abilities for Geralt. There are a lot of segments where you investigate bodies or locations for clues, and all of them are well-done and logical for a man who has superhuman senses and reflexes. The game doesn't really do a good job of explaining the crafting system, but craft the Witcher gear and keep the monster parts from the beasts you slay. 

Get this game. It's a masterpiece and one that might not be surpassed anytime soon. I've done 4 full runs + DLCs and I'm still fiending for more.

review:
180 Hours.

Thats how long it took to finish the game, and both expansions.

I went out of my way to do every single witcher contract. Every Sidequest. Everything.

This game made me OBSESSED about the world I was playing in. And the simple act of just... being a Witcher was also shockingly fun. Because 7/10, you're being hired to fight and kill monsters, whether or not you kill them, how you find and fight them... all of that is left in your control.

The game does not feel like it is wasting your time. The pacing is amazing, the optional things are truly optional. The voice acting is superb, as is the facial expressions and mocap work. The villains are believable, the flaws in heroic characters are as well. You can play the horny witcher and bang every sorceress you find (Just dont try that with Triss and Yen specifically, it will horribly backfire)

And blood and wine, blood and wine is the MOST FUN ive had in an expansion. One that makes the "happily ever after" ending feel EARNED. and now just given.


And on top of all of that, the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ MODDING COMMUNITY man! You want to get back into this and shake things up, you can find mods for just about every ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ part of the game. Including adding the systems from previous titles in the series.

This, is not a fast game. This is not a game youre going to finish in a week. Give this series the time, and it will take you on a magical ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ journey.

Good luck on the Path.

review:
The Witcher: Uma Obra que Transcende o Entretenimento
Existe uma categoria rara de obras que ultrapassa a função para qual foi criada e se torna, ela própria, um objeto de reflexão cultural. The Witcher, franquia nascida dos livros do polonês Andrzej Sapkowski e brilhantemente adaptada pelo estúdio CD Projekt Red em sua série de jogos, é exatamente isso.
Quando iniciei minha jornada por esse universo, o que me capturou não foi apenas a mecânica de combate ou os gráficos impressionantes — foi a densidade narrativa de um mundo construído com rigor quase acadêmico. Sapkowski não criou uma fantasia de heróis imaculados. Ele criou um mundo onde o bem e o mal coexistem de forma ambígua, onde cada escolha moral tem peso real, e onde os personagens carregam contradições que os tornam profundamente humanos. Geralt de Rívia, o bruxo protagonista, não é um herói — é um agente num mundo complexo tentando sobreviver com alguma dignidade. Isso ressoa muito comigo.
O aspecto que mais me impressiona na trilogia, especialmente em The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt, é o cuidado com o worldbuilding histórico e etnográfico. Cada região do jogo — Novigrad, Velen, as Ilhas Skellige — possui sua própria identidade cultural, seus próprios costumes, folclore e tensões sociais. Skellige, por exemplo, é uma representação extraordinária da cultura nórdica pré-cristã, com seus clãs, seus códigos de honra e sua cosmologia própria. Quem tem interesse em história e em como identidades culturais se constroem ao longo do tempo encontrará aqui um material riquíssimo para reflexão.
O folclore eslavo presente na série — as leshens, as wili, os espectros que habitam pântanos e florestas — dialoga diretamente com tradições orais que muitas vezes são negligenciadas pela cultura pop dominante. É uma validação de que culturas não hegemônicas têm profundidade mitológica suficiente para sustentar narrativas universais. Não é diferente do que vejo no mundo guarani: uma cosmologia riquíssima que merece o mesmo reconhecimento.
A qualidade da escrita é outro elemento que me cativa. Os diálogos são inteligentes, os personagens secundários têm arcos próprios, e missões aparentemente menores guardam histórias tão elaboradas quanto a trama principal. Em The Witcher 3, a expansão Blood and Wine constrói uma crítica à ilusão da utopia com uma sofisticação que rivaliza com boa literatura. Toussaint, o reino "perfeito" da expansão, é uma metáfora sobre como beleza superficial pode esconder violência sistêmica — um tema absolutamente contemporâneo.
Do ponto de vista técnico e artístico, os jogos da CD Projekt Red estabeleceram padrões que a indústria ainda tenta alcançar. A trilha sonora, as paisagens, o design de personagens — tudo comunica uma coerência estética que denota respeito pelo público. Não é um produto feito para consumo rápido. É uma obra que convida à imersão, à contemplação e, por vezes, ao desconforto necessário.
The Witcher me lembra por que a ficção especulativa, quando bem executada, é uma das formas mais poderosas de se explorar a condição humana. É um jogo — mas também é história, filosofia, antropologia e arte.

Recomendo sem hesitar a qualquer pessoa que acredite que o entretenimento pode, e deve, elevar quem o consome.

review_english: The Witcher A Work that Transcends Entertainment There is a rare category of works that goes beyond the function for which it was created and becomes, in itself, an object of cultural reflection. The Witcher, a franchise born from the books of Polish author Andrzej Sapkowski and brilliantly adapted by studio CD Projekt Red into its series of games, is exactly that. When I started my journey through this universe, what captured me wasn't just the combat mechanics or the impressive graphics, it was the narrative density of a world built with almost academic rigor. Sapkowski did not create a fantasy of immaculate heroes. He created a world where good and evil coexist ambiguously, where every moral choice has real weight, and where the characters carry contradictions that make them deeply human. Geralt of Rivia, the protagonist witcher, is not a hero, he is an agent in a complex world trying to survive with some dignity. This resonates with me a lot. The aspect that impresses me most in the trilogy, especially in The Witcher 3 Wild Hunt, is the care taken with historical and ethnographic worldbuilding. Each region of the game Novigrad, Velen, the Skellige Islands has its own cultural identity, its own customs, folklore and social tensions. Skellige, for example, is an extraordinary representation of pre-Christian Norse culture, with its clans, its codes of honor and its own cosmology. Anyone interested in history and how cultural identities are constructed over time will find here rich material for reflection. The Slavic folklore present in the series, the leshens, the wili, the specters that inhabit swamps and forests, dialogues directly with oral traditions that are often neglected by dominant pop culture. It is a validation that non-hegemonic cultures have enough mythological depth to support universal narratives. It is no different from what I see in the Guarani world, a very rich cosmology that deserves the same recognition. The quality of the writing is another element that captivates me. The dialogues are intelligent, the secondary characters have their own arcs, and seemingly minor missions have stories as elaborate as the main plot. In The Witcher 3, the Blood and Wine expansion constructs a critique of the illusion of utopia with a sophistication that rivals good literature. Toussaint, the "perfect" kingdom of expansion, is a metaphor for how superficial beauty can hide systemic violence, an absolutely contemporary theme. From a technical and artistic point of view, CD Projekt Red's games set standards that the industry is still trying to reach. The soundtrack, the landscapes, the character design all communicate an aesthetic coherence that denotes respect for the audience. It is not a product made for quick consumption. It is a work that invites immersion, contemplation and, at times, necessary discomfort. The Witcher reminds me why speculative fiction, when done well, is one of the most powerful ways to explore the human condition. It's a game but it's also history, philosophy, anthropology and art. I recommend it without hesitation to anyone who believes that entertainment can, and should, elevate those who consume it.

review: Dlouhou dobu jsem se na tuto hru chystal a četl ty kvanta neskutečných recenzí vychvalujících hru do nebes. Po prvních okamžicích jsem byl v šoku, jak je dementně udělaná kamera, neustálé korigování pohledu myší, to opravdu je strašné a otravné. Souboje alá Tekken, What the hell is that??? Jako souboje celou hru založené na tom, že do stran skáču, jak cirkusák, tak promiňte, to nemám chuť hrát. A inventář a vůbec build postavy, také bída, takže hru zase odinstaluji a nechám ji v propadlišti dějin, což mě mrzí, ale to se prostě jinak nedá...

After the first moments, I was in shock, how demented the camera is, the constant correction of the mouse's view, it's really terrible and annoying. Fights à la Tekken, What the hell is that??? Like fights the whole game based on jumping to the sides, like a circus performer, so sorry, I don't feel like playing that. And the inventory and the build of the characters in general is also miserable, so I will uninstall the game again and leave it in the abyss of history, which makes me sad, but there is simply no other way...

review:
Tavsiye için kararsızlık olsa basacam. Oyunun combat kısmı kötü, galiba optimizasyon problemi de var grafikler bazen saçmalıyor, karakter burnundan şarap içiyor filan. Bazen fps çöküyor 10 a kadar düşüyor, menüde gezdikten sonra oyuna girince. Hikayesi güzel fakat çok uzun, bir kızı 25 saat arıyosun ve işin kötü yanı milletin piyonu oluyosun görev veriyolar yapıyosun görev veriyolar yapıyosun. Gereksiz uzun bir oyun(Rol yapma oyunu olmasına rağmen). Oyunun iyi yanları atmosferi, karakterleri, yenilenmiş grafikleri bunlar iyi ama oyunu tamamlayamamış. O kadar övüldüğü için almak isterseniz almayın çünkü orta çağ teması ve fantezi temasını sevenlerin bayıla ayıla oynadığı bir oyun olmuş, bence o yüzden bu kadar çok övülüyor. Tema benim hoşuma gitmediği için oyunu sadece hikayesi için oynadım. Ki zaten hikayesi kitaptan uyarlama. Yine de uzun vadeli oynanabilecek bir oyun. Yan görevlerine hiç bakmadım yorum yok. Seviye sistemi gereksiz.

review_english: If I'm indecisive about the recommendation, I'll press on it. The combat part of the game is bad, I think there is also an optimization problem, the graphics sometimes look ridiculous, the character drinks wine through his nose, etc. Sometimes the fps crashes and drops to 10 when you enter the game after browsing the menu. The story is good but very long, you search for a girl for 25 hours and the worst part is that you become the nation's pawn, they give you tasks, you do them, they give you tasks and you do them. An unnecessarily long game (even though it's a role-playing game). The good aspects of the game are its atmosphere, characters, and renewed graphics. These are good, but they could not complete the game. If you want to buy it because it is so praised, do not buy it, because it is a game that those who love the medieval theme and fantasy theme love to play, I think that is why it is praised so much. Since I didn't like the theme, I played the game only for the story. The story is adapted from the book anyway. Still, it's a game that can be played long term. I've never looked at the side missions, no comments. The level system is unnecessary. Note: It is a game where you complete the task you received from the man who gave you the task, go to the task man, finish the task you received from that man, and tell the other task men that you received it.

review:
Truly a work of art and a labour of love. I've been playing this masterpiece for over ten years now.
It defined me as a young man and continues to entertain me to this day. There is still always something to discover and enjoy. The DLCs are phenomenal and I'm overjoyed that they are still releasing new content for the game after so many years have passed, with the announcement of the "songs of the past" DLC.

review:
Я не проходил трилогию «Ведьмака» — я прожил её.

От грязных трактиров первой части до ледяных берегов Скеллиге в The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt. Пока другие искали новую игру, я снова седлал Плотву и возвращался туда, где каждый квест помню почти наизусть.

Сотни часов спустя я понял: Геральт был не персонажем, а попутчиком. А лучший комплимент игре — не пройти её один раз, а раз за разом находить повод вернуться обратно. ⚔️🐺

Пока другие искали новую игру, я снова седлал Плотву и возвращался туда, где каждый квест помню почти наизусть. Сотни часов спустя я понял Геральт был не персонажем, а попутчиком. А лучший комплимент игре не пройти её один раз, а раз за разом находить повод вернуться обратно.
review_english: I didn't go through the Witcher trilogy, I lived it. From the dirty inns of the first game to the icy shores of Skellige in The Witcher 3 Wild Hunt. While others were looking for a new game, I again saddled Roach and returned to where I remember every quest almost by heart. Hundreds of hours later, I realized Geralt was not a character, but a fellow traveler. And the best compliment to a game is not to beat it once, but to find a reason to come back over and over again.

review:
对我而言，巫师三是一部优缺点极其明显的神作，以下我将主观地谈谈我对这部作品的体验。

优点:巫师3的剧情几乎无可挑剔，角色塑造和情节分支是史诗级的，昆特牌非常简单好玩，风景和配乐不落窠臼(陶森特的风光谁能拒绝)，dlc的质量极高，老婆和女儿非常美，开放世界也是一大特色（当然我不认为巫师三是彻底的开放世界，他有别于上古卷轴5的彻底自由，是杰洛特的相对自由）

缺点:战斗系统过于简单粗暴，玩法流派十分有限，原版boss战过于简单（还好dlc的boss质量很高），节奏较慢导致很容易劝退新玩家，以及糟糕的骑马系统和传送系统。

总而言之，在如今这个时代，这款游戏依旧是值得购买体验的杰作，这将是我玩过性价比最高的游戏之一，但在我心中的地位不如同类型的«上古卷轴5:天际»，期待«巫师4»的发布能给我更大的惊喜

review_english: For me, The Witcher 3 is a masterpiece with extremely obvious advantages and disadvantages. Below I will talk about my experience of this work subjectively. Advantages: The plot of The Witcher 3 is almost impeccable. The character creation and plot branches are epic. Gwent is very simple and fun. The scenery and soundtrack are unconventional (who can resist the scenery of Toussaint). The quality of DLC is extremely high. My wife and daughter are very beautiful. The open world is also a major feature. Of course, I don’t think The Witcher 3 is a complete open world. It is different from the complete freedom of The Elder Scrolls 5. It is the relative freedom of Geralt. Disadvantages: The combat system is too simple and rough. The gameplay genre is very limited. The original boss battle is too simple. Fortunately, the bosses in the DLC are of high quality. The slow pace makes it easy to discourage new players, as well as the poor horse riding system and teleportation system. All in all, in this era, this game is still a masterpiece worth buying and experiencing. It will be one of the most cost-effective games I have ever played, but its status in my heart is not as good as the same type of The Elder Scrolls 5 Skyrim. Looking forward to The Witcher 4. The release of will give me a bigger surprise

review:
Jogo bom pra crlh, principalmente para quem gosta de história, escolhas e mundo aberto.

O jogo tem muitas missões interessantes, e várias delas parecem tão bem feitas quanto a história principal. As escolhas que você faz realmente importam e podem mudar o rumo de algumas situações.

O mundo é grande, bonito e cheio de lugares para explorar, monstros para enfrentar e personagens marcantes. O Geralt também é um protagonista muito bom, com um jeito sério, mas ao mesmo tempo carismático.

No começo, a jogabilidade pode parecer um pouco confusa, principalmente por causa dos menus e sistemas do jogo. Mas depois que você se acostuma, a experiência fica muito melhor.

Na minha opinião, The Witcher 3 é um dos melhores RPGs já feitos. Vale muito a pena para quem gosta de uma história forte e de passar muitas horas explorando.

review_english: Good game for kids, especially for those who like history, choices and an open world. The game has a lot of interesting missions, and several of them feel as well done as the main story. The choices you make really matter and can change the course of some situations. The world is big, beautiful and full of places to explore, monsters to fight and memorable characters. Geralt is also a very good protagonist, with a serious but at the same time charismatic manner. At first, the gameplay can seem a little confusing, mainly because of the game's menus and systems. But once you get used to it, the experience gets much better. In my opinion, The Witcher 3 is one of the best RPGs ever made. It's well worth it for those who like a strong story and spending many hours exploring.

review:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Obra-prima dos RPGs

The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt é, sem dúvida, um dos melhores RPGs já criados. A jornada de Geralt de Rívia oferece uma combinação quase perfeita entre narrativa, exploração, combate e desenvolvimento de personagens.

O mundo aberto é gigantesco, vivo e repleto de atividades interessantes. Diferente de muitos jogos do gênero, até mesmo as missões secundárias possuem histórias bem escritas, personagens memoráveis e escolhas que realmente fazem o jogador refletir sobre suas decisões.

A ambientação é fantástica, com cenários impressionantes, trilha sonora marcante e uma atmosfera medieval sombria que prende a atenção do início ao fim. A exploração é extremamente recompensadora, sempre existindo algo novo para descobrir em cada canto do mapa.

O sistema de combate é divertido e estratégico, exigindo o uso correto de espadas, sinais, poções e óleos para enfrentar monstros e inimigos cada vez mais desafiadores. Além disso, a progressão do personagem permite criar diferentes estilos de jogo.

Outro destaque são as expansões Hearts of Stone e Blood and Wine, que possuem qualidade comparável — ou até superior — a muitos jogos completos disponíveis no mercado.

Mesmo anos após seu lançamento, The Witcher 3 continua sendo uma referência para a indústria dos videogames. Uma aventura emocionante, repleta de personagens inesquecíveis, escolhas impactantes e momentos épicos.

Recomendado para qualquer fã de RPG, fantasia medieval e histórias bem contadas. Uma verdadeira obra-prima. 🐺⚔️🍷

review_english: Masterpiece of RPGs The Witcher 3 Wild Hunt is, without a doubt, one of the best RPGs ever created. Geralt of Rivia's journey offers a near-perfect combination of narrative, exploration, combat, and character development. The open world is gigantic, alive and full of interesting activities. Unlike many games of the genre, even the side missions have well-written stories, memorable characters and choices that really make the player reflect on their decisions. The setting is fantastic, with impressive scenery, a striking soundtrack and a dark medieval atmosphere that holds your attention from start to finish. Exploration is extremely rewarding, with there always being something new to discover in every corner of the map. The combat system is fun and strategic, requiring the correct use of swords, signs, potions and oils to face increasingly challenging monsters and enemies. Additionally, character progression allows you to create different play styles. Another highlight are the Hearts of Stone and Blood and Wine expansions, which are of comparable quality or even higher than many complete games available on the market. Even years after its release, The Witcher 3 continues to be a reference for the video game industry. An exciting adventure, full of unforgettable characters, impactful choices and epic moments. Recommended for any fan of RPG, medieval fantasy and well-told stories. A true masterpiece.

review: My favorite open world story game so far. The setting, the characters, the world, the atmosphere, the soundtrack, etc. is just one of the best of its kind. What sets this game apart from other bangers like rdr2 for me is especially the setting and the world. The moment you enter Velen for the first time, the game just keeps going with fascinating and captivating the player. Every inch of this world is so enjoyable to explore and astonishing on its own and the story.... oh the story. The main quest is beautifully written and just never gets boring. it might be a bit complicated when you are not familiar with the world of the witcher and its previous parts but let me ensure you, you still can play this gem completely blind. It basically can be considered a stand-alone title. Besides the main story, even the completely optional side story are better then most main storyline in other games. The Questline of the Bloody Baron is up until today a dear memory of mine since its so complex and really shocked me. It left me there sitting like "♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥, was this the right decision?" So yeah, if you havent played this Masterpiece and deserved GOTY yet, what are you wating for. They could even demand 60 and i would still gladly buy it. And by the way. the 2 dlcs are a MUST BUY. so get the GOTY edition and not the standard one. Especially Blood and Wine itself is as big as a whole new game and adds a totally unique new storyline as mysterious and captivating like the rest. GET THE GAME!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

review: Лучшая игра десятилетия, скорее всего даже за все время. Отличный сюжет, подогревающий длительный интерес к игре и постоянно держащий в предвкушении "что же будет дальше?". Настолько прекрасно проработаны персонажи, что даже нпс чувствуются "живыми". Постоянные шутки от главных и второстепенных персонажей, ирония, сарказм. В целом, считаю, что персонажи в этой игре являются одним из ее главных достоинств. Я не видел игры, в которой настолько хорошо будут проработаны персонажи. Атмосфера во время игры вообще заслуживает отдельной статьи.  Шедевральная музыка, прекрасно подходящая под атмосферу, персонажей, события. После 4.0 картинка стала великолепной, способна потягаться со многими современными проектами в плане графики. Огромное множество выборов на протяжении игры на вкус и цвет каждого - от ведьмака-пацифиста до ведьмака-геноцидника. Возможность "одевать" Геральта как захочешь: хочешь, чтобы он ходил в шмотках с нпс - пожалуйста; хочешь собрать один из кучи сетов - пожалуйста. Также множество билдов на вкачку умений на Геральта: маг, алхимик, воин, всевозможные миксы. Помимо основного сюжета отлично проработаны доп. задания и ведьмачьи заказы, многие из которых не дадут заскучать, если захочешь разбавить сюжет. Куда же без всеми любимого гвинта, хоть сиди с каждым попавшимся играй. Возможность нажраться, посетить бордель, переспать с кучей чародеек, постоянный мат являются ко всему перечисленному приятным бонусом. Даже не знаю, что сказать о длс, они просто прекрасны. 2 дополнительные интереснейшие истории, которые некоторым зайдут даже больше, чем основная сюжетка. В общем и целом, эта игра стоит каждую потраченную на нее копейку даже без скидок. Игра, которую хочется перепроходить снова и снова. Даже если ее оставить на время, вернувшись в ведьмака, можно снова засесть за компом на неделю, играя только в этот шедевр.

Возможность нажраться, посетить бордель, переспать с кучей чародеек, постоянный мат являются ко всему перечисленному приятным бонусом. Даже не знаю, что сказать о длс, они просто прекрасны. 2 дополнительные интереснейшие истории, которые некоторым зайдут даже больше, чем основная сюжетка. В общем и целом, эта игра стоит каждую потраченную на нее копейку даже без скидок. Игра, которую хочется перепроходить снова и снова. Даже если ее оставить на время, вернувшись в ведьмака, можно снова засесть за компом на неделю, играя только в этот шедевр.
review_english: The best game of the decade, most likely even of all time. An excellent plot that fuels long-term interest in the game and constantly keeps you in anticipation of “what will happen next?” The characters are so well developed that even the NPCs feel “alive”. Constant jokes from the main and secondary characters, irony, sarcasm. Overall, I think the characters in this game are one of its main strengths. I haven't seen a game where the characters are so well developed. The atmosphere during the game generally deserves a separate article. Masterpiece music, perfectly suited to the atmosphere, characters, events. After 4.0 the picture became magnificent, able to compete with many modern projects in terms of graphics. A huge variety of choices throughout the game to suit everyone’s tastes and colors - from a pacifist witcher to a genocidal witcher. The ability to “dress” Geralt the way you want, if you want him to wear clothes with NPCs - please, if you want to collect one of a bunch of sets - please. There are also many builds for pumping up Geralt’s skills: mage, alchemist, warrior, all kinds of mixes. In addition to the main plot, the extras are well developed. tasks and witcher orders, many of which will not let you get bored if you want to dilute the plot. Where would we be without everyone’s favorite gwent? At least sit and play with everyone you come across. The opportunity to get drunk, visit a brothel, sleep with a bunch of sorceresses, and constant swearing are a pleasant bonus to all of the above. I don’t even know what to say about the DLS, they are just beautiful. 2 additional interesting stories that some will enjoy even more than the main plot. Overall, this game is worth every penny you spend on it, even without discounts. A game that you want to play again and again. Even if you leave it for a while, returning to the Witcher, you can sit down at the computer again for a week, playing only this masterpiece.

review:
One of the best games I have ever played. I have never been more immersed in a story than when I was playing Witcher 3. The main story is great, but I would argue that the side quests and contracts are even better. There are memorable characters all over, and your choices through the story affect how they interact with you. This also includes the different creatures and monsters, all of which have interesting lore that often is inspired by real folklore. Besides the story and the world, the combat is also pretty fun, and it gives you several playstyles to choose from. This is just describing the main game, but the DLCs are EVEN BETTER! 

Buy it all and thank me later!

review:
The Witcher 3 é, para mim, a melhor história já feita em um videogame. E não estou falando apenas da campanha principal, mas também das missões secundárias, que são tão bem escritas e desenvolvidas que muitas vezes parecem fazer parte da história principal.

Cada personagem tem personalidade, cada escolha tem consequências e cada missão consegue prender sua atenção de um jeito diferente. É impressionante como até as tarefas mais simples possuem uma narrativa envolvente e memorável.

O mundo do jogo é vivo, rico em detalhes e cheio de histórias para contar. Você começa uma missão achando que será algo rápido e, de repente, está envolvido em uma trama tão interessante quanto a campanha principal.

Poucos jogos conseguem alcançar o nível de escrita, construção de personagens e narrativa que The Witcher 3 apresenta. Para mim, ele continua sendo uma referência quando o assunto é contar uma boa história nos videogames.

Simplesmente uma obra-prima. 🐺⚔️🎮

review_english: The Witcher 3 is, for me, the best story ever made in a video game. And I'm not just talking about the main campaign, but also the side missions, which are so well written and developed that they often feel like they're part of the main story. Each character has a personality, each choice has consequences and each mission manages to hold your attention in a different way. It's impressive how even the simplest tasks have an engaging and memorable narrative. The game world is alive, rich in details and full of stories to tell. You start a mission thinking it will be something quick and, suddenly, you are involved in a plot as interesting as the main campaign. Few games can reach the level of writing, character building and narrative that The Witcher 3 presents. For me, he continues to be a reference when it comes to telling a good story in video games. Simply a masterpiece.

review: Well. My first ever singleplayer game (simulator games not included) to reach 50 hours. And yeah it was great. The game came out in 2015 so one feels its age, it is janky, you will get frustrated, the horse is even worse at time, and the combat is okay, not good, not bad just okay. But where the game ironically shines is the visuals and the environments, how a 11 year old game can look so good made me speechless. You have a massive map one that would take 100+ hours to fully explore, it stays fresh mostly, and ofcourse 2 expansion packs. I just finished the base game (51.2 hours it took me) , I'm jumping to Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 and Cyberpunk 2077 now, but best believe I WILL BE BACK TO COMPLETE THE EXPANSIONS. ONE THING WHERE THIS GAME DOES SHINE AND WHAT CARRIES IT ULTIMATELY IS THE STORY ITS SO DAMN GOOD. 36 Diff endings technically but with the main characters about 7. Anyways I digress, if you enjoy open world games with a very very good story, choices that matter, and a massive and beautiful world filled with content and are fine dealing with the jankiness any 11 year old game will suffer off, and the combat, which although not amazing I did like it, then be my guest. This game will not dissapoint!

review:
This is not just my favorite RPG. This is probably my favorite game, period.

The world is ridiculous in the best way. You ride into some muddy village thinking you’ll clear one tiny question mark, and three hours later you’re dealing with a cursed family drama, a monster with better lore than most main villains, a morally impossible choice, and somehow a quick round of Gwent because apparently Geralt has time for cards while the world is falling apart.

That’s what makes it special. The side quests don’t feel like filler. They feel like short stories with real characters, ugly choices, and endings that don’t always let you feel clean. The world is beautiful, sad, funny, cruel, and alive. Velen, Novigrad, Skellige, Toussaint — every place has its own mood, music, problems, and people you remember.

Geralt is also one of the best main characters in games. Tired, dry, dangerous, secretly soft, and constantly dragged into everyone else’s terrible life decisions. The writing, music, atmosphere, monsters, DLCs, and little quiet moments all come together into something that still feels huge and personal at the same time.

I love this game. Not “yeah, it’s good” love. More like “I was supposed to do one quest and now it’s 3 AM and I’m emotionally attached to a horse, a bard, and a deck of cards” love. Absolute masterpiece.

Got The Witcher 3 when it was only like 10 euros (including the Hearts of Stone and Blood and Wine expansion)

Honestly one of the best games I have played so far. Great story (with important decision making), graphics, music, playability, etc...

Haven't played in other difficulties other than Normal, but can say it gives the player a decent challenge without compromising the story.

Overall would recommend it to anyone. 

9/10

review:
At first I want to point out, how beautifully detailed this game is. For example I was incredibly astonished that a rescued archer of a quest was suddenly an ally in the endgame. (I don’t want to spoiler more…) 

In fact every decision has an impact to the course of the game. I would therefore recommend making decisions thoughtfully and at times with empathy. Just because I harmed a character, that decision meant a particular quest wasn’t even available to begin with, and I was therefore denied an achievement. For that reason, I’ve decided to start the game from scratch for the third time.

Although I’m already completed the story, it’s still exciting to start afresh. After all, the game offers a variety of playstyles based on the character’s development. Plus, when starting a new game, you can of course choose a school of sorcery you’re not yet familiar with.

I wish especially the newer players a lot of fun, exploring this breathtaking open world of this never getting old masterpiece as I did.

review:
⚔️ MISTROVSKÉ DÍLO, KTERÉ NEMÁ KONKURENCI ⚔️

CZ - Zaklínač 3: Divoký hon je pro mě absolutní srdcovka a dost možná nejlepší hra, jakou jsem kdy hrál. Svět, který CD Projekt RED vytvořil, vás kompletně pohltí. Příběh Geralta z Rivie je napsaný tak geniálně, že i obyčejné vedlejší úkoly mají lepší děj než celé jiné hry. Postavy jako Yennefer, Ciri nebo Triss vám přirostou k srdci a na tenhle zážitek jen tak nezapomenete.

A co se týče DLC (Srdce z kamene / O víně a krvi)? To nejsou jen nějaké levné přídavky. Jsou to v podstatě celé další hry, které kvalitou a atmosférou (zejména pohádkový Toussaint) posouvají laťku ještě výš.

Tuhle hru by si měl zahrát povinně každý, kdo to s hraním myslí vážně. Chytlo mě to natolik, že jsem v tom nechal kus života a pokořil všech 78 achievementů na 100 %. 10/10 a dal bych i víc, kdyby to šlo.

⚔️ A MASTERPIECE THAT HAS NO EQUAL ⚔️

ENG - The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt is an absolute masterpiece and quite possibly the best game I have ever played. The world that CD Projekt RED created completely consumes you. Geralt of Rivia's story is so brilliantly written that even simple side quests have better plots than entire other games. Characters like Yennefer, Ciri, or Triss will truly grow on you, and it's an experience you won't easily forget.

And as for the DLCs (Hearts of Stone / Blood and Wine)? These aren't just cheap add-ons. They are basically entire new games that push the bar even higher with their quality and atmosphere (especially the fairytale-like Toussaint).

This game is a must-play for anyone who takes gaming seriously. I loved it so much that I dedicated a huge chunk of my time to it and unlocked all 78 achievements for a 100% completion. 10/10, and I’d give it more if I could.

review:
Ca y'est, je viens enfin de terminer l'aventure principale de the Witcher 3 après environ 120 heures passées dans le jeu, et quelle aventure ! Je l'ai débuté avec en tête de découvrir un classique du médium pour un prix dérisoire en soldes, et j'en ressort un peu amoureuse (de son monde, de ses personnages, de sa narration). Ce jeu ne démérite pas à mes yeux de sa réputation, et je suis sous le charme.


[h3] Quelques défauts de maniabilité [/h3]

[i]Mais commençons cette critique par les défauts qu'on peut lui imputer. Car the Witcher 3 n'en est pas dépourvu, et je peux en un sens comprendre ceux qui n'adhérent pas à la proposition. Simplement, ces défauts n'ont pas affecté mon expérience globale, et passent au second plan de mon appréciation. [/i]

Oui, les contrôles sont parfois un peu rigides - surtout à cheval - et ce serait mentir que de dire que je n'ai jamais été frustrée par la maniabilité ou les passages un peu 'parkour'. J'ai parfois eu envie de jeter ma souris ou ma manette contre l'écran après m'être retrouvée coincée par un mur invisible dans une zone de relief, incapable de rejoindre l'endroit indiqué sur la carte qui se trouve clairement à portée de vue, ou essayé en vain de grimper sur ce putain de bateau, ou m'être retrouvé coincée contre un mur ou un pont à cheval au milieu d'une course ou d'un moment d'histoire important. (Vraiment, la conduite à cheval, autant j'ai vécu de sublimes moments d'exploration très exaltant, autant j'ai parfois eu envie d'envoyer Roach au bûcher). Clairement tout n'est pas parfait de ce point de vue là, et je peux comprendre que certains trouvent ça rédhibitoire. 

[i]Mais j'ai vécu tout ces problèmes comme un léger désagrément parsemant une aventure au combien immersive, à la narration soignée, dans un monde magnifique, au côté d'un personnage incroyable. Je me suis laissée emportée par l'historie et l'univers du jeu, et ce que j'y ai vu / vécu m'a beaucoup parlé [/i].


[h3]Une aventure immersive et inoubliable [/h3]

Déjà, je trouve le monde de Witcher III sublime. Je ne parlerai pas de qualités graphiques à proprement parler - je joue sur un ordinateur portable en qualité basse pour que le jeu tourne au mieux - mais j'ai très souvent eu le souffle coupé par la beauté des paysages. Je ne compte plus le nombre de fois où je me suis juste arrêté pour admirer la vue. Même Velen, qui n'est pas des plus accueillantes avec ses marécages et ses terres désolées par la guerre, peut révéler des charmes cachés dans les lumières du couchant. (La météo de ce jeu, comme son cycle jour / nuit, donnent parfois des panoramas inoubliables). De manière générale, je trouve que le jeu excelle dans l'art d'établir une ambiance pour un lieu. Les marécages hostiles de Velen ne dégagent pas du tout la même atmosphère que l'intrigue mafieuse et politique de Novigrad (surtout la nuit), ou que les champs de tournesols qu'on trouve au Nord de la ville. Et que dire de Skellige ? J'ai passé mes premières heures sur l'île à m'extasier devant chaque falaise, et la majesté de ses montagnes enneigées. Si certains déplacements, surtout en altitude, sont parfois un peu frustrant à Skellige, qu'est-ce que ces îles sont belles, et comme le contraste entre chaque zones est rafraîchissant. 

Si je suis amoureuse de la beauté et l'aspect très immersif / authentique de l'open world, l'écriture mérite elle aussi d'être encensée. Déjà par le soin qui lui a clairement été donné. Chaque cinématique, même pour une quête annexe, propose une mise en scène qui va plus loin que le simple champ/contre-champ, avec parfois des plans très 'cinématique', justement. Les quêtes secondaires et contrats sont parfois drôles, parfois plus sombres, mais questionnent souvent le rôle du Witcher dans ce monde, souvent assez moche et cruel. J'aime beaucoup tout l'aspect moral grise du personnage et des choix qu'il est possible de faire. Au travers de ces quêtes, j'ai fait de ma version de Geralt un sorceleur un peu désabusé, qui penche parfois du côté des monstres opprimés, lui même conscient de ce qu'est d'être un "freak" dans un monde qui pratique la chasse aux sorcières. 

Geralt de Riv fait d'ailleurs aujourd'hui parti de mes protagonistes de jeu vidéo favoris. Comment pourrait-il en être autrement ? Le doublage (anglais dans mon cas) est fantastique, il est terriblement cool lorsqu'il est temps de dézinguer du monstre, mais plus sensible qu'il en à l'air derrière sa façade bourrue. (Sa relation paternelle avec Ciri est subtile mais touchante, et j'ai adoré suivre sa romance avec Yennefer, ou son amitié avec Dandelion). Geralt est très plaisant à suivre, et c'est un plaisir de voyager dans cet univers avec lui.


[i]Bref, j'ai pris mon temps pour terminer l'intrigue principale, qui si elle n'est pas parfaite en terme de rythme, s'est terminé sur un gros moment d'émotions. Je suis maintenant impatiente d'aller plus haut vers le Nord, découvrir les nouveaux tableaux d'affichages, et entamer Hearts of Stone. J'ai vraiment adoré mon temps dans Witcher 3, et tant d'aventures m'attendent encore ...

Because the Witcher 3 is not devoid of it, and I can in a sense understand those who do not adhere to the proposition. Simply, these flaws did not affect my overall experience, and take a back seat to my appreciation. i Yes, the controls are sometimes a little stiff - especially on horseback - and it would be a lie to say that I was never frustrated by the handling or the somewhat 'parkour' passages. I sometimes wanted to throw my mouse or controller at the screen after finding myself stuck by an invisible wall in an area of ​​relief, unable to get to the place marked on the map that was clearly within sight, or trying in vain to climb onto the damn boat, or getting stuck against a wall or a horse bridge in the middle of a race or an important story moment. (Really, riding a horse, as much as I experienced sublime moments of very exhilarating exploration, I sometimes wanted to send Roach to the stake). Clearly everything is not perfect from this point of view, and I can understand that some people find it prohibitive. i But I experienced all these problems as a slight inconvenience dotting an extremely immersive adventure, with careful storytelling, in a magnificent world, alongside an incredible character. I let myself be carried away by the story and the world of the game, and what I saw there spoke to me a lot. h3 An immersive and unforgettable adventure h3 Already, I find the world of Witcher III sublime. I won't talk about graphic qualities strictly speaking - I play on a laptop at low quality so that the game runs at its best - but I have very often had my breath taken away by the beauty of the landscapes. I can't count the number of times I've just stopped to admire the view. Even Velen, which is not the most welcoming with its swamps and war-wasted lands, can reveal hidden charms in the sunset lights. (The weather in this game, like its day-night cycle, sometimes gives unforgettable panoramas). Generally speaking, I find that the game excels in the art of establishing a mood for a location. The hostile swamps of Velen do not at all have the same atmosphere as the mafia and political intrigue of Novigrad (especially at night), or the sunflower fields found to the north of the city. And what about Skellige? I spent my first hours on the island marveling at each cliff, and the majesty of its snow-capped mountains. If certain movements, especially at altitude, are sometimes a little frustrating in Skellige, how beautiful these islands are, and how refreshing the contrast between each area is. If I am in love with the beauty and the very immersive authentic aspect of the open world, the writing also deserves to be praised. Already by the care that was clearly given to it. Each cutscene, even for a side quest, offers a staging that goes further than the simple reverse shot, sometimes with very 'cinematic' shots, in fact. The side quests and contracts are sometimes funny, sometimes darker, but often question the role of the Witcher in this world, often quite ugly and cruel. I really like the whole gray moral aspect of the character and the choices that can be made. Through these quests, I made my version of Geralt a slightly disillusioned witcher, who sometimes leans towards the oppressed monsters, himself aware of what it is to be a "freak" in a world that practices witch hunting. Geralt of Rivia is now one of my favorite video game protagonists. How could it be otherwise? The dubbing (English in my case) is fantastic, he's terribly cool when it's time to take down the monster, but more sensitive than he appears behind his gruff facade. (His paternal relationship with Ciri is subtle but touching, and I loved following his romance with Yennefer, or his friendship with Dandelion). Geralt is very pleasant to follow, and it is a pleasure to travel in this universe with him. i In short, I took my time to finish the main plot, which if not perfect in terms of rhythm, ended with a big emotional moment. I am now impatient to go further north, discover the new tabs display waters, and start Hearts of Stone. I absolutely loved my time in Witcher 3, and so many adventures still await me...

review:
Редко какая игра способна настолько органично сочетать глубокий сюжет, проработанный мир, живых персонажей и чувство настоящего приключения. *The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt* — именно такой случай. На момент написания этого отзыва я нахожусь на Скеллиге, и уже сейчас могу с полной уверенностью сказать, что это одна из лучших игр, в которые мне доводилось играть.
Главное достоинство игры — её удивительная способность погружать в собственную вселенную. Каждый квест, даже самый незначительный на первый взгляд, обладает собственной драматургией, моральной неоднозначностью и последствиями. Здесь нет банального разделения на добро и зло — есть лишь сложные решения, каждое из которых оставляет след в судьбах людей и самого мира.
Особенно впечатлил Скеллиге. Суровый архипелаг, омываемый свинцовыми волнами, поражает своей атмосферой. Величественные горные пейзажи, древние традиции островитян, политические интриги и легенды, пронизывающие каждый уголок этих земель, создают ощущение настоящего живого мира. Исследовать эти острова — одно удовольствие.
Отдельной похвалы заслуживают персонажи. Геральт здесь не просто главный герой, а полноценная личность со своими убеждениями и принципами. Второстепенные герои также написаны с редким мастерством: их мотивация понятна, а поступки выглядят естественными и правдоподобными.
Визуальная составляющая даже спустя годы остаётся впечатляющей. Закаты над морем Скеллиге, густые леса Велена и оживлённые улицы Новиграда способны соперничать со многими современными проектами. Музыкальное сопровождение заслуживает отдельных оваций: композиции не просто сопровождают происходящее, а усиливают эмоциональное воздействие каждой сцены.
Конечно, игра не лишена мелких шероховатостей. Иногда встречаются незначительные технические огрехи или спорные элементы боевой системы. Однако на фоне масштаба и качества всего остального эти недостатки выглядят практически незаметными.
The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt — это не просто игра, а выдающееся произведение интерактивного искусства, демонстрирующее, каким должен быть настоящий ролевой проект. Она увлекает, восхищает и заставляет искренне переживать за происходящее.
Оценка: 10/10. Безоговорочный шедевр, который обязан пройти каждый поклонник RPG.
