review:
Jogar The Witcher 3 anos após o lançamento só prova o quanto este jogo é atemporal. Mesmo com o peso da idade em alguns pequenos aspectos técnicos, a jornada de Geralt de Rívia continua engolindo a maioria dos RPGs modernos com extrema facilidade.

O que torna o jogo inesquecível:
Narrativa Impecável: Até as missões secundárias mais simples têm roteiros absurdamente bem escritos, com escolhas morais cinzentas que realmente fazem você parar e pensar.

Construção de Mundo: Um universo vivo, denso e melancólico. Explorar o Continente — de Velen a Skellige — é uma experiência incrivelmente imersiva.

Personagens Marcantes: A dinâmica entre o Geralt, a Ciri e a Yennefer (ou Triss) carrega o jogo com um peso emocional raríssimo de encontrar na indústria.

Se você, assim como eu, demorou anos para dar uma chance a este clássico, não espere mais. É um daqueles raros jogos que deixam um "vazio" enorme quando terminam. Uma obra-prima obrigatória para qualquer fã de videogames.

Nota: 10/10 (E não deixe de jogar as DLCs, Hearts of Stone e Blood and Wine, que conseguem ser tão boas ou até melhores que a campanha principal).
review_clean: Jogar The Witcher 3 anos após o lançamento só prova o quanto este jogo é atemporal. Mesmo com o peso da idade em alguns pequenos aspectos técnicos, a jornada de Geralt de Rívia continua engolindo a maioria dos RPGs modernos com extrema facilidade. O que torna o jogo inesquecível Narrativa Impecável Até as missões secundárias mais simples têm roteiros absurdamente bem escritos, com escolhas morais cinzentas que realmente fazem você parar e pensar. Construção de Mundo Um universo vivo, denso e melancólico. Explorar o Continente de Velen a Skellige é uma experiência incrivelmente imersiva. Personagens Marcantes A dinâmica entre o Geralt, a Ciri e a Yennefer (ou Triss) carrega o jogo com um peso emocional raríssimo de encontrar na indústria. Se você, assim como eu, demorou anos para dar uma chance a este clássico, não espere mais. É um daqueles raros jogos que deixam um "vazio" enorme quando terminam. Uma obra-prima obrigatória para qualquer fã de videogames. Nota 10 10 (E não deixe de jogar as DLCs, Hearts of Stone e Blood and Wine, que conseguem ser tão boas ou até melhores que a campanha principal).
review_english: Playing The Witcher 3 years after its release only proves how timeless this game is. Even with the weight of age in some small technical aspects, Geralt of Rivia's journey continues to swallow most modern RPGs with extreme ease. What makes the game unforgettable Flawless Narrative Even the simplest side missions have absurdly well-written scripts, with gray moral choices that really make you stop and think. World Building A living, dense and melancholic universe. Exploring the Continent from Velen to Skellige is an incredibly immersive experience. Striking Characters The dynamic between Geralt, Ciri and Yennefer (or Triss) carries the game with an emotional weight that is very rare to find in the industry. If you, like me, took years to give this classic a chance, don't wait any longer. It's one of those rare games that leaves a huge "void" when it ends. A must-have masterpiece for any video game fan. Rating 10 10 (And don't forget to play the DLCs, Hearts of Stone and Blood and Wine, which can be as good or even better than the main campaign).

Among the advantages, you can single out quite beautiful graphics (for its age) and quite good optimization. P.S. This is my subjective opinion at the moment. Of course, it will change as the plot and the gameplay as a whole develop.

I cannot say enything about this game, that hasn't been said already.

I will just say, that after playing the entire trilogy and reading all the books, reaching the end of the final DlC, was one of the greatest experiences I have ever had in gaming. 

All I am saying is, this is the game that I recomend to people that don't play games as a getaway, and there is a reason for that. The story of the White Wolf really leaves an impact on most people. Perhaps you will feel it to.

If there is a list of games that everyone should play, then the wither 3, is definitely there!

review:
从22年玩到现在玩了至少300个小时，本体通了两边，石之心和血与酒也分别在24年和26年通关，支线和委托做了绝大部分，剩下的支线和委托等哪天想玩了再上去看看吧，我相信巫师的故事远远未结束。
这是一个极具沉浸感的剑与魔法的世界，主线是贯穿故事的中心，但是支线和委托任务却非常细腻的描绘和诠释出了巫师世界的点点滴滴，这才是巫师3的神奇和优秀之处！
最后的一点支线和委托等巫师4快出的时候再玩！

review_english: I have played it for at least 300 hours since 2022. I have completed both sides of the main body. Hearts of Stone and Blood and Wine were also completed in 2024 and 26 respectively. Most of the side quests and commissions are done. Let’s check out the remaining side quests and commissions when you want to play them someday. I believe that the story of the wizard is far from over. This is a very immersive world of swords and magic. The main plot is the center of the story, but the side plots and commissions very delicately depict and interpret every bit of the wizarding world. This is the magic and excellence of The Witcher 3. The last few branches and commissions will be played when The Witcher 4 is released soon.

review:
Open world RPG costruito attorno a una cosa molto semplice: raccontare storie fatte bene.

Il loop di gioco è quello classico — esplorazione, combattimenti, investigazione — ma il vero valore non sta nel sistema in sé, quanto in quello che succede mentre lo usi. Ogni quest, anche secondaria, è scritta come se fosse importante. E spesso lo è.

Il combat system è funzionale, a tratti ripetitivo, e non è mai stato il punto forte del gioco. Ma regge il tutto senza mai diventare un vero problema, perché viene costantemente spinto in secondo piano dalla qualità della narrazione e del mondo.

L’open world è enorme, ma soprattutto “vivo” nella percezione: villaggi, strade, mostri e NPC non sono solo contenuto, ma pezzi di un contesto coerente. Alcune zone sono più riuscite di altre, ma l’insieme mantiene sempre una forte identità.

Il vero salto di qualità è nella scrittura: missioni principali e secondarie hanno spesso più peso emotivo di interi giochi. I DLC alzano ulteriormente l’asticella, con contenuti che tranquillamente potrebbero essere giochi a sé.

Non è un gioco perfetto. Il combat resta semplice, alcune meccaniche RPG sono grezze e l’open world a tratti si dilata troppo. Ma quando funziona, funziona a un livello che pochi altri titoli raggiungono.

È uno di quei giochi che non si valutano solo per “come sono fatti”, ma per cosa ti lasciano addosso quando smetti di giocarci.

review_english: Open world RPG built around a very simple thing about telling stories done well. The game loop is the classic one of exploration, fighting, investigation but the real value lies not in the system itself, but in what happens while you use it. Every quest, even secondary, is written as if it were important. And it often is. The combat system is functional, sometimes repetitive, and has never been the strong point of the game. But it holds together without ever becoming a real problem, because it is constantly pushed into the background by the quality of the narrative and the world. The open world is enormous, but above all alive in the perception villages, roads, monsters and NPCs are not just content, but pieces of a coherent context. Some areas are more successful than others, but the whole always maintains a strong identity. The real leap in quality is in the writing, main and secondary missions often have more emotional weight than entire games. The DLCs raise the bar even further, with content that could easily be a game in itself. It's not a perfect game. The combat remains simple, some RPG mechanics are crude and the open world expands too much at times. But when it works, it works at a level that few other titles reach. It's one of those games that aren't evaluated just for how they're made, but for what they leave behind when you stop playing them.

review:
The Witcher 3 is a massive, story-driven RPG that’s easy to get lost in - though not without some fatigue along the way. I originally came into it as my first Witcher game, and with that I had no trouble following the story, as it does a great job getting you up to speed. The narrative is one of its biggest strengths, with strong characters and a more focused, personal approach compared to something like Skyrim. While I still prefer the freedom and character customization of Skyrim, this is a very worthy entry in the action fantasy genre.

From a technical standpoint, it’s impressive. The world looks great, runs smoothly, and generally feels polished. Combat is solid, and monster hunting is easily the highlight of the entire experience - it’s where the game feels most focused and rewarding. Riding horseback, on the other hand, felt a bit clunky, and I often preferred traveling on foot just to avoid the hassle.

Where the game starts to stumble is in its sheer size. There’s so much content here that it can feel overwhelming. Side quests are plentiful - sometimes to a fault - and while many are well-crafted, others begin to feel repetitive or like filler. I’d complete several quests only to unlock even more, which eventually led to burnout. The main story also starts strong but loses some momentum in its later stages, which made it easier to drift away into side content instead of pushing forward. Another minor gripe is the lack of environmental variety. While the world is beautifully designed, much of it shares a similar look and tone, lacking the kind of biome diversity that makes exploration feel more dynamic.

Still, despite these issues, The Witcher 3 is undeniably a great game. It’s ambitious, content-rich, and full of memorable moments. It just might be too much of a good thing at times.

Nowadays, I often just fire it up to play Gwent for an hour or two. I think it's definitely my favorite in-game game ever developed. I even own a set IRL.
